claims.text: Existing PFNs, when interpreted as Bayesian ATE estimators, can exhibit prior-induced confounding bias.
claims.source_quote: We show that existing PFNs, when interpreted as Bayesian ATE estimators, can exhibit prior-induced confounding bias...

claims.text: The prior in existing PFNs is not asymptotically overwritten by data.
claims.source_quote: ...the prior is not asymptotically overwritten by data...

claims.text: The prior in existing PFNs prevents frequentist consistency.
The OSPC helps to restore frequentist consistency in PFN-based estimators.
claims.source_quote: We show that the OSPC helps to restore frequentist consistency...

claims.text: The OSPC can yield a semi-parametric Bernstein-von Mises theorem for calibrated PFNs.
claims.source_quote: ...and can yield a semi-parametric Bernstein-von Mises theorem for calibrated PFNs...

claims.text: Both the calibrated PFN-based estimators and the classical semi-parametric efficient estimators converge in distribution with growing data size.
claims.source_quote: ...both the calibrated PFN-based estimators and the classical semi-parametric efficient estimators converge in distribution with growing data size.

claims.text: OSPC is implemented through tailoring martingale posteriors on top of the PFNs.
claims.source_quote: Finally, we implement OSPC through tailoring martingale posteriors on top of the PFNs.

claims.text: PFNs calibrated with martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that asymptotically matches frequentist uncertainty.
claims.source_quote: ...PFNs calibrated with our martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that (i) asymptotically matches frequentist uncertainty...

claims.text: PFNs calibrated with martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that is well calibrated in finite samples in comparison to other Bayesian ATE estimators.
claims.source_quote: ...and (ii) is well calibrated in finite samples in comparison to other Bayesian ATE estimators.
